Specialty Networks’ PPS Analytics platform analyzes data from electronic medical records (EMR), practice management, imaging, and dispensing systems and transforms it into meaningful and actionable insights for providers and other stakeholders by using artificial intelligence and modern data analytics capabilities.
...
The Senior Data/AI Engineer is a senior individual contributor on the Data & AI Engineering team responsible for leading the design and delivery of data and AI solutions across our Lakehouse and SQL Server environments. The responsibility of the role is to own projects from problem framing through scoping, architecture, prototyping, and production deployment.
...
Work spans modern healthcare data platforms and AI engineering, including medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold), Delta Lake, T-SQL ETL, and natural language AI capabilities such as NLP/NLU, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), document-level LLM extraction, and agentic frameworks applied to EHR/EMR, practice management (PM), pharmacy, claims, and clinical note data.
...
Support the design and delivery of data and AI solutions across Lakehouse and SQL Server environments.
...

...
Design and build natural language AI capabilities such as NLP/NLU, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), document-level LLM extraction, and agentic frameworks.
...
Apply AI and data engineering solutions to diverse healthcare data, including EHR/EMR, practice management (PM), pharmacy, claims, and clinical notes.
...
Leverage modern AI-assisted development tooling (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) to accelerate delivery.
...
Experience: 4+ years in Data Engineering, Software Engineering, or Analytics, with a proven track record of taking 0-to-1 initiatives from concept to production.
...
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Analytics, Engineering, or a related field highly preferred; Master’s degree a plus.
...
Data Platform Expertise: 3+ years of hands-on experience with Databricks and/or Snowflake, a major cloud platform (Azure, GCP, or AWS), PySpark, Spark SQL, and Delta Lake fundamentals (ACID, MERGE, OPTIMIZE/ZORDER, schema evolution).
...
AI/ML/NLP Production Experience: Proven experience (with healthcare data context) deploying NLP/NLU and modern AI/LLM-based systems (e.g., RAG, document-level extraction) from research through production, covering chunking, retrieval, prompting, evaluation, monitoring, and cost/performance tuning. Experience with agentic frameworks is a plus.
...
AI-Assisted Development: Hands-on experience using Claude Code, Codex, or similar AI-assisted development tools.

highlights:

...
I’m an Generative AI Engineer with 4+ years of experience in building and deploying AI, NLP, and LLM-based solutions across healthcare, finance, and enterprise analytics. Expertise in developing LLM-powered applications using Azure OpenAI (GPT-4), OpenAI API, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, LangChain, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines, leveraging embeddings and vector search (FAISS, Pinecone) to improve grounded, context-aware responses. Strong experience in prompt engineering, LLM evaluation, and transformer fine-tuning using LoRA, QLoRA, and PEFT to optimize domain adaptation and inference performance. Skilled in end-to-end ML development, including data preprocessing, feature engineering, model training, and performance tuning using Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, and NLP techniques using spaCy and NLTK. Proficient in deploying AI services through FastAPI/Flask, containerization using Docker, and scalable deployment support using Kubernetes, with MLOps and workflow support using MLflow, Airflow, and Databricks.

...
I am a passionate Data Scientist and Machine Learning Engineer with experience designing, developing, and deploying end-to-end AI/ML and Generative AI solutions across cloud platforms including AWS, GCP, and Azure. I specialize in transforming complex data into scalable, intelligent systems that drive business value and improve decision-making. My expertise spans Machine Learning, Deep Learning, NLP, Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Agentic AI systems, and multi-agent orchestration. I have hands-on experience building production-grade AI applications using technologies such as Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Spark, Kafka, LangGraph, AutoGen, vector databases, and MLOps frameworks. I have built and optimized scalable data pipelines and AI solutions involving real-time analytics, predictive modeling, recommendation systems, fraud detection, forecasting, conversational AI, and autonomous workflows. My experience includes working with distributed systems, cloud-native architectures, REST APIs, microservices, and deploying enterprise AI applications using modern DevOps and MLOps practices. I enjoy solving challenging problems at the intersection of Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Big Data, and Cloud Engineering, while collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver impactful solutions.

### Data Scientist at [Cardinal Health](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cardinal-health) (Current)
...
As a Data Scientist with a proven track record at Cardinal Health and previous roles at Wells Fargo and IBM, I specialize in leveraging Python machine learning techniques to solve complex business challenges. My expertise spans a wide range of domains, including demand forecasting, price optimization, and email classification, where I have successfully implemented advanced algorithms to drive strategic decision-making and improve operational efficiency. I have also excelled in tackling unstructured data problems, such as fuzzy matching in pharmaceutical analytics, where precision and accuracy are paramount. Leveraging my proficiency in NLP and deep learning, I've developed innovative solutions to automate tasks and extract meaningful insights from large datasets. Moreover, my experience extends to advanced modeling techniques like stacking modeling, enabling me to build robust predictive models that outperform traditional approaches. With a keen eye for detail and a passion for driving business impact through data-driven insights, I thrive in dynamic environments where innovation and collaboration are valued. Show less
...
As a Data Scientist, I led a transformative project in price optimization, employing machine learning, Python, and deep learning to enhance sales performance and maximize profit margins. Our goal was to determine the best pricing strategy for products, balancing competitiveness with profitability by offering discounts to customers strategically. Using advanced machine learning algorithms, we analyzed historical sales data, market trends, and customer behavior to predict optimal pricing for each product category. Dynamic pricing adjustments were recommended in real-time based on factors such as customer segmentation, purchasing patterns, and competitor pricing. Beyond price optimization, our project encompassed various components to improve overall sales performance and customer satisfaction. We conducted analysis of purchase correlations to identify complementary products, implemented predictive models to reduce churn rates, and utilized fuzzy matching techniques for recommending discounted pharmaceutical products. In terms of technology, we leveraged Google Cloud Platform (GCP), BigQuery, and VeetexAi for data storage, processing, and text analysis. Docker was used for containerization, facilitating seamless deployment of our solution into production environments. The project's impact was significant, delivering a measured ROI of $5 million by increasing sales revenue while maintaining healthy profit margins. This project demonstrates my ability to leverage data science techniques and technology to drive tangible business outcomes, and I'm eager to apply my expertise to future challenges. Show less

Building dashboards and visualization layers using Power BI and Tableau, making insights accessible to stakeholders across clinical and operational teams. Collaborating closely with cross-functional teams to translate business needs into scalable analytics applications, aligning machine learning solutions with enterprise objectives and operational efficiency goals.

A few years ago, being a data engineer primarily involved building ETL pipelines and tuning Spark jobs. 

Today, the landscape has shifted significantly. We are now focused on designing retrieval systems and context pipelines that effectively feed large language models (LLMs). 

The world of data engineering is evolving rapidly, and I have explored what lies ahead in my latest article. 

The Future of Data Engineering in the Age of LLMs: From Pipelines to Prompts

results.title: Abdillahi Mohamed
highlights:
Data Scientist | ML Practitioner | MS Computer
...
(Machine Learning), Georgia Tech
...
Data Scientist at [Cardinal Health](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cardinal-health)
...
I build ML models and data infrastructure that drive revenue and strategy decisions at scale. At Cardinal Health, I develop market sizing frameworks, propensity scoring models, and customer segmentation systems using PU Learning, XGBoost, and segment-based statistical methods on BigQuery. I've built end-to-end pipelines from raw data through model development to Tableau dashboard delivery for executive decision-making, including work that identified significant untapped market opportunity across enterprise customer segments. I'm completing an MS in Computer Science with a Machine Learning specialization at Georgia Tech, where I've implemented VAEs, GANs, and denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPMs) from scratch in PyTorch, built deep learning systems for image classification (ResNet, focal loss), and worked with NLP/transformer architectures including beam search, BPE tokenization, and self-attention. Previously at Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS), I applied NLP and text-mining techniques to improve search relevance across unstructured scientific literature, reducing irrelevant results by 80%. Python (PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn, pandas) · SQL (BigQuery, PostgreSQL, MS SQL) · R · Tableau · Vertex AI · XGBoost · Apache Spark · AWS
...
#### Data Scientist (Current)
...
Built ML-driven market sizing frameworks to quantify total addressable market (TAM), market share, and growth opportunity across enterprise customer segments. Developed propensity scoring and lookalike models using PU Learning and XGBoost to identify high-value acquisition targets. Designed segment-based benchmarking methodology after validating that direct regression approaches (Random Forest, XGBoost) failed due to extreme within-segment variance and multicollinearity, a pivot that produced actionable results where standard ML did not. Built and maintained BigQuery pipeline infrastructure integrating internal and external data sources. Designed Tableau dashboard systems delivering market opportunity analysis and prioritized target lists to commercial leadership. Presented methodology and findings to senior leadership through executive briefings. Key tools: Python, BigQuery, XGBoost, Vertex AI, Tableau, SQL.

providing predictive analytics and personalized dashboards
...
Platform leverages artificial intelligence to provide predictive analytics with personalized dashboards that provide actionable insights for contract compliance, cost controls and product alternatives. Employs machine learning with upstream logistics data and downstream clinical data to rapidly respond to changes in patient medication needs.
...
Cardinal Health's AI Center of Excellence was formed in 2021 and is led by director Anagha Vyas. The center identifies promising AI use cases, tests solutions for delivering anticipated results, and works to accelerate the use of AI in digital and commercial technologies across the healthcare supply chain.
...
Cardinal Health partners with Palantir Technologies to deploy AI and machine learning through the Foundry platform, combining clinical data with real-time customer purchasing data. This creates a clinically integrated supply chain that provides insights for contract compliance, cost controls, and product alternatives.
...
Cardinal Health uses the Jvion CORE platform integrated into their Navista TS system to analyze Social Determinants of Health (SDoH) data. This AI tool helps identify high-risk patient profiles and has demonstrated results like an 18% reduction in emergency department visits at participating cancer centers.
...
Cardinal Health emphasizes human oversight as critical to AI success and focuses on balancing AI promise with responsible use. They ensure every AI project begins with an unmet customer need, verify data is 'AI-ready,' and confirm AI fits desirably into the customer experience before implementation.
...

# Lead Scientist, Artificial Intelligence Research
...
The Senior Engineer, AI is responsible for building AI and Machine learning models and pipelines, with a focus on generative AI, LLMs and predictive modelling. The successful candidate will work closely with data scientists and product leads to implement AI models, build required data and code pipelines through rigorous engineering disciplines You should have expertise in the design, development, management, and maintenance of systems and handling of large datasets. You should have hands on experience with common machine learning and AI frameworks, and a good knowledge of AI and machine learning fundamentals (deep learning, regression, classification and clustering algorithms), retrieval augmented generation pipelines, as well as a good grasp of the design and evaluation of LLM-supported use cases.
...

- Automate infrastructure used by the Data Science team

Design and develop large language models, generative AI systems, and NLP pipelines for a Fortune 25 healthcare company, with responsibility for end-to-end ML model lifecycle from development through production deployment.
...
### Position Title
...
Gen AI Engineer
...
### Job Description
...
Gen AI Engineer
...
The Gen AI Engineer is responsible for analyzing and modeling organizational data for the Artificial Intelligence (AI) function to draw business insights, which can be used to make business decisions.
...
- Applies data extraction, transformation and loading techniques in order to connect large data sets from a variety of sources.
...
- LLM development and fine-tuning strategies, best practices, and standards to enhance AI ML model deployment and monitoring efficiency.
...
- Develop roadmap and strategy for NLP, LLM, Gen AI model development and lifecycle implementation.
...
- Responsible for the design and development of custom ML, Gen AI, NLP, LLM Models for batch and stream processing-based AI ML pipelines including data ingestion, preprocessing modules, search and retrieval, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), NLP/LLM model development and ensure the end-to-end solution meets all technical and business requirements, and SLA specifications.
...
- Identify and implement model optimizations to
...
system efficiency.
...
- Collaborate closely with the MLOps, product teams, business stakeholders, machine learning engineers, and software engineers for the deployment of machine learning models into production environments, ensuring smooth integration, reliability and scalability.
...
- Ensure the use of standards, governance and best practices in ML model development, and adherence to model and data governance standards.
...
### Minimum Requirements
...
- Requires a Bachelor’s degree in a highly quantitative field (Computer Science, Machine Learning, Operational Research, Statistics, Mathematics, etc.) or equivalent degree and 4 or more years of experience; or any combination of education and experience in configuration management, which would provide an equivalent background.
...
### Preferred Skills, Capabilities, and Experiences
...
- Advanced Python proficiency.
- 4+ years of professional hands-on experience leveraging large sets of structured and unstructured data to develop data-driven tactical and strategic analytics and insights using ML, NLP, and computer vision solutions.
...
- Demonstrated 4+ years hands-on experience with Python, SQL, Hugging Face, TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, and Spark.
...
- Experience with GCP/AWS cloud platforms.
- Strong knowledge of and measurable hands-on experience with developing or tuning Large Language Models (LLM) and Generative AI (GAI)
...
- Experience with NLP, LLMs (extractive and generative), fine-tuning and LLM model development.
...
- Experience developing and optimizing high-quality prompts for NLP applications.
...
- LLM Infrastructure & Deployment: LLM serving platforms (vLLM, Text Generation Inference, FastAPI); Model quantization for LLMs (GPTQ, AWQ, bitsandbytes); GPU memory optimization techniques (tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism); LLM caching strategies for inference optimization; RAG architecture design and implementation.
...
- Advanced cloud infrastructure (AWS EKS/ECS, GCP GKE, Azure AKS) knowledge.
...
- Containerization strategies for ML workloads; Canary deployments for ML models.
...

# AI/NLP Engineer Remote (Ohio, USA) Contract
...
Job Title: AI/NLP Engineer / Developer
...
### Location: Remote (Ohio, USA)
...
Prior experience with Cardinal is highly preferred.
...
Job Overview: We are seeking an experienced AI/NLP Engineer to design, develop, and integrate advanced AI solutions leveraging Azure Cognitive Services, Large Language Models (LLMs), and NLP frameworks. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience with Databricks, SQL Server, and modern AI/ML pipelines, capable of building intelligent, scalable, and production-ready solutions.
...
- Design and implement AI and NLP-based solutions integrated with Azure Cognitive Services and Databricks.
- Develop, fine-tune, and deploy LLMs and NLP models using frameworks like PyTorch, LangChain, and OpenAI APIs.
- Work with offline open-weight models and integrate them within enterprise data workflows.
...
- Implement Claude API, OpenAI API, and other third-party model integrations for conversational and generative AI use cases.
...
- Architect and automate data engineering pipelines to support ML/AI workloads.
...
- Integrate Databricks Foundational Models into enterprise data ecosystems for model training, inference, and monitoring.
...
- Collaborate with data scientists, engineers, and architects to ensure model scalability, performance, and compliance.
...
- Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or a related field.
- Overall IT Experience: 8-12 Years
- Expertise in Python and NLP model development (Transformers, LLMs, etc.).
- Strong experience with Azure Cognitive Services integration.
- Hands-on experience with Databricks and SQL Server.
- Experience with Offline Open Weight Models and Databricks Foundational Model Integration.
- Proficiency in Claude API, OpenAI API, LangChain, and PyTorch.
...
- Knowledge of AI/ML architecture and data pipeline design for scalable solutions.
...
- Experience with Vector Databases (FAISS, Pinecone, etc.).
- Familiarity with MLOps, Model Monitoring, and LLM fine-tuning.
- Exposure to Azure Machine Learning, Kubernetes, and Docker for deployment.
- Understanding of Responsible AI principles, model governance, and security.
